bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Machine Learning for Biological Data

Deep learning, protein language models, GNNs, and foundation models applied to biological sequence and structure.

54 papers curated · High: 6 · Medium: 48 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

Molecular therapy. Nucleic acids 2026-Sep-08 Medium

Enhancing protein expression in humans through codon optimization with transformer and contrastive learning.

This paper introduces COformer, a deep learning framework that uses transformer and contrastive learning to optimize codons for enhanced protein expression in human cells. Unlike traditional frequency-based methods, COformer captures sequence context to improve translation efficiency and mRNA stability, which is critical for developing effective mRNA vaccines and therapies.

machine-learningdeep-learningtool
Scientific reports 2026-Jun-30 Medium

An optimized dual-branch method for DNA enhancer identification based on pretrained models and multi-scale local regulatory motif extraction.

This paper presents iEnhancer-Hybrid, a computational method that combines a pretrained DNA language model (DNABERT-2) with a multi-scale local motif extraction branch to improve the identification of DNA enhancer sequences. The dual-branch architecture addresses the limitation of DNABERT-2 in capturing position-sensitive local patterns like transcription factor binding sites, achieving more accurate enhancer prediction.

deep-learninggenomicstool
Scientific reports 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Deep learning enables rabies virus detection and antibody quantification in cell culture.

This paper presents the first AI-based workflow for detecting rabies virus and quantifying antibodies in cell culture, using deep learning models like ResNet, Swin Transformer V2, and MambaOut to automate fluorescence image analysis. The approach reduces manual labor and subjectivity, potentially improving scalability and accuracy in rabies diagnostics and antibody quantification.

deep-learningmachine-learningtool
Journal of medical Internet research 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Quality Evaluation of Large Language Model-Assisted Generation of Initial Senior Physician Ward Round Records for Patients With Acute Poisoning: Cross-Sectional Study.

This study evaluates the quality of initial senior physician ward round records generated by large language models (LLMs) for patients with acute poisoning. The findings indicate that LLMs can produce clinically acceptable initial records, but their quality varies and requires human oversight. This work highlights both the potential and limitations of using LLMs for generating critical clinical documentation in emergency settings.

machine-learningclinicaltool
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Enhancing Cross-Context Generalization in Drug Perturbation Prediction with a Multimodal Conditional Diffusion Framework.

This paper introduces a multimodal conditional diffusion framework that integrates diverse biological data types to predict drug-induced gene expression changes. The model significantly improves generalization to unseen drugs and cell lines compared to existing methods, addressing a key limitation in precision medicine.

machine-learningdrug-discoverytranscriptomics
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jun-30 Medium

OTalign: Optimal Transport Alignment for Remote Protein Homologs Using Protein Language Model Embeddings.

This paper presents OTalign, a new method that uses Optimal Transport theory to align protein sequences, specifically targeting the difficult problem of aligning remote homologs with very low sequence identity. By leveraging protein language model embeddings, the approach provides a mathematically rigorous way to handle residue matches and gaps, improving alignment accuracy for distantly related proteins.

toolprotein-structuremachine-learning
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-26 High

SPEAK: Spatial Prompting with Expert Aligned Knowledge for Tissue Domain Identification in Spatial Transcriptomics.

SPEAK is a new method that uses large language models (LLMs) and expert knowledge to identify tissue domains in spatial transcriptomics data. It creates spatial context prompts for each cell based on its neighbors' cell types and marker genes, enabling zero-shot inference and expert-guided fine-tuning.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsLLM
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-25 Medium

NanoCellAnnotator: Formalizing Expert Cell Type Annotation with Large Language Models.

NanoCellAnnotator uses large language models to improve cell-type annotation in spatial transcriptomics by constraining predictions to biologically supported cell types, reducing hallucinations. It is designed to run locally, addressing reproducibility and privacy concerns associated with cloud-hosted models. This tool formalizes expert knowledge integration for more reliable annotation.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsLLM
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-24 Medium

trAIt: Species-by-Trait Data Retrieval using Large Language Models.

trAIt is a new software tool that uses a large language model to automatically retrieve species traits from scientific literature in the Europe PubMed Central database, saving researchers time compared to manual literature searches. It features a graphical user interface where users can specify species and traits of interest, and it combines information from multiple papers using a consensus-based summarization approach.

toolLLMdatabase
Research square 2026-Jun-19 Medium

MutexaGPT: An Intuition-to-Design Translator for Physics-based Enzyme Engineering.

MutexaGPT is a multi-agent LLM platform that translates plain-English descriptions of enzyme engineering intuition into physics-based simulations and variant designs. It bridges the gap between qualitative human insights and quantitative, actionable design principles for enzyme engineering.

deep-learningLLMtool
Chemical science 2026-Jun-18 Medium

HCGT-PL: a heterogeneous contrastive graph transformer unifying protein-ligand affinity prediction and structure-based virtual screening.

The authors developed HCGT-PL, a new deep learning framework that represents protein-ligand complexes as directed heterogeneous graphs and uses contrastive learning to improve binding affinity prediction and virtual screening. The model outperforms existing methods on diverse benchmarks by better capturing complex molecular interactions.

machine-learningdrug-discoverymethodology
Research square 2026-Jun-18 High

Heavy-chain immune repertoire sequencing enables language-model prediction of antigen-specific antibodies.

This study demonstrates that antigen-specific antibodies can be predicted directly from mRNA-derived heavy-chain V(D)J sequencing data by fine-tuning a protein language model. This approach enables rapid identification of binding-specific antibodies from unselected immune repertoires, which could accelerate therapeutic antibody discovery and immune monitoring.

deep-learningLLMimmunology
Kardiologia polska 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Predicting 3-year mortality after myocardial infarction in a structured care program: A comparative analysis of Cox regression and machine learning models.

This study compares machine learning models with traditional Cox regression for predicting 3-year mortality after myocardial infarction in a structured care program, finding that machine learning did not significantly outperform the conventional statistical approach. The results suggest that in real-world clinical settings, simpler models may be sufficient for risk stratification.

machine-learningclinical
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-08 Medium

Machine learning reveals mechanisms and feedstock effects on potassium and magnesium uptake by wheat in biochar-amended soil in greenhouse.

This study uses machine learning to predict how different biochar feedstocks affect potassium and magnesium uptake in wheat roots, revealing complex interactions between biochar properties, soil biology, and plant traits. The findings help clarify feedstock-dependent mechanisms, offering insights for optimizing biochar use in agriculture.

machine-learningplantsmethodology
Cell reports methods 2026-Jul-08 High

GenART: Reading the genome's language with adaptive "words".

GenART is a new genomic language model that learns to automatically break DNA sequences into meaningful variable-length 'words' without needing manual annotation. This adaptive tokenization approach outperforms existing methods across multiple genomic tasks by capturing biologically relevant boundaries from sequence context.

machine-learningdeep-learninggenomics
BMC biology 2026-Jul-07 Medium

Phylogenetic analysis and machine learning identify signatures of selection and predict deleterious mutations in common bean (Phaseolus vulgaris L.).

This study combines phylogenetic analysis and machine learning to identify signatures of selection and predict deleterious mutations in common bean. The findings suggest that domestication and selection can purge mildly deleterious mutations, thereby increasing the accumulation of favorable alleles in breeding populations.

machine-learningevolutionplants
Scientific data 2026-Jul-07 High

A comprehensive dataset of 32 million pentapeptide structures for high-throughput virtual screening.

Researchers generated an open dataset of 32 million 3D structures covering all possible pentapeptide sequences (3.2 million unique sequences, each with up to 10 conformers). This resource enables high-throughput virtual screening and structure-based analysis of small peptides without needing experimental structures.

protein-structuredatabasedrug-discovery
SLAS technology 2026-Jul-07 Medium

AI/ML-Enabled Multi-Omics Integration of Host Genetics, Immunity, and the Gut Microbiome in Crohn's Disease: From Diagnosis to Theranostics.

This review examines how AI and machine learning can integrate multi-omics data (genomics, proteomics, transcriptomics, and gut microbiome) to better understand Crohn's disease pathogenesis and improve diagnosis and personalized treatment. The authors argue that current diagnostic and therapeutic approaches are limited by clinical variability and lack of specific biomarkers, and that AI-driven multi-omics integration offers a path toward more precise, theranostic strategies.

machine-learningmulti-omicsmicrobiome
Journal of cheminformatics 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Smiles-based bioactivity prediction through molecular encoder selection and data augmentation.

This study presents a machine learning pipeline that selects optimal molecular encoders and applies data augmentation to predict IC50 values from SMILES strings, developed for a drug discovery competition. The approach focuses on improving bioactivity prediction accuracy for ASK1 inhibitors, demonstrating a practical workflow for early-stage drug discovery.

machine-learningdrug-discoverytool
BMC genomics 2026-Jul-06 Medium

A self-attention-based deep learning model for identifying key genes in insect pupal metamorphosis.

This study developed a deep learning model combining Word2vec embeddings and self-attention mechanisms to identify key genes involved in insect pupal metamorphosis. The model was trained on protein sequences from 293 insect species across 14 orders and can calculate gene importance weights to pinpoint metamorphosis-related genes.

deep-learningevolutiontool
BMC microbiology 2026-Jul-06 Medium

A population-based retrospective machine learning study of COVID-19 severity using integrated clinical and viral genomic data in Jiangsu Province, China.

This study used machine learning to analyze clinical and viral genomic data from COVID-19 patients in Jiangsu Province, China, identifying key factors associated with severe disease. The findings highlight region-specific risk determinants that can inform local public health strategies for managing COVID-19 in the endemic phase.

machine-learningclinicalgenomics
Advanced science (Weinheim, Baden-Wurttemberg, Germany) 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Harnessing Large-Scale Multi-Omics Data for Risk Prediction and Deep Phenotyping of Valvular Heart Diseases in the General Population.

This study uses UK Biobank data to build risk prediction models for valvular heart disease (VHD) by combining clinical, proteomic, and metabolomic data. The best performing models were Cox proportional hazards models using key clinical factors, achieving good predictive accuracy (C-index 0.75-0.81). The work also explores potential biological mechanisms underlying VHD and its subtypes.

proteomicsmetabolomicsclinical
Microbiome 2026-Jul-06 High

Conserved 3' stem-loop structures enable comprehensive analysis of bacterial transcription termination in metagenomes.

This study identifies conserved 3' stem-loop structures that allow for comprehensive detection of both Rho-independent and Rho-dependent transcription terminators in bacterial metagenomes, overcoming previous limitations that focused only on model species. The approach enables systematic analysis of transcription termination across diverse bacterial communities, revealing previously hidden regulatory diversity.

metagenomicsmicrobiomegenomics
BMC bioinformatics 2026-Jul-06 Medium

A multi-view feature fusion framework with interpretable graph convolution for predicting microbe-drug associations.

This paper presents IDEAL, a new computational framework that uses graph convolution and multi-view feature fusion to predict associations between human microbes and drugs. The key innovation is that the model is designed to be interpretable, allowing researchers to understand which features drive its predictions, addressing a major limitation of existing 'black box' approaches. This could aid drug development and precision medicine by revealing the biological interaction mechanisms between microbes and drugs.

machine-learningdrug-discoverymicrobiome
Nature communications 2026-Jul-06 Medium

AI-guided CRISPR screening reveals therapeutic targets in psoriasis.

This study used a genome-wide CRISPR screen combined with a machine learning tool called VirtualCRISPR to identify regulators of the IL-17 receptor in keratinocytes, the key cells in psoriasis lesions. The approach uncovered potential therapeutic targets that could lead to the development of topical small-molecule treatments for psoriasis, offering an alternative to current systemic biologics.

CRISPRmachine-learningdrug-discovery
BMC cancer 2026-Jul-04 Medium

Machine learning-driven development of a novel unfolded protein response-related gene signature for predicting lung adenocarcinoma patient prognosis.

This study developed a gene signature based on the unfolded protein response (UPR) pathway to predict prognosis in lung adenocarcinoma patients. Using multiple machine learning algorithms and public datasets, the authors created a UPR-related risk score that was integrated into a clinical nomogram for patient stratification.

cancermachine-learningtranscriptomics
BMC medical imaging 2026-Jul-04 Medium

End-to-end 2.5D multisequence-multichannel fusion model for preoperative survival prediction in glioma: a retrospective study.

This study developed an end-to-end deep learning model that fuses multiple MRI sequences to predict 24-month survival in glioma patients before surgery. The model outperformed standard approaches by systematically optimizing the combination of imaging channels and network depth.

deep-learningcancerclinical
Nature communications 2026-Jul-04 Medium

Positional interpretation of cis-regulatory code and nucleosome organization with deep learning models.

This paper introduces PISA, a deep learning-based method that interprets cis-regulatory sequence rules and nucleosome organization by attributing contributions of specific DNA bases to genomic readouts like MNase-seq. PISA helps disentangle true biological signals from experimental biases by visualizing how transcription factor motifs influence nucleosome positioning.

deep-learninggenomicsmethodology
Journal of molecular modeling 2026-Jul-04 Medium

SMASH: Screening Molecules Accurately on Small Hardware. Fast, user-friendly, enhanced with a machine learning virtual screening tool.

SMASH is a free, open-source virtual screening tool that improves upon its predecessor by using machine learning to predict binding sites, automatically determine titration states, and accelerate molecular docking with GPU processing. It combines machine learning with energy scoring functions and K-means clustering, enabling efficient handling of large projects on local hardware.

drug-discoverymachine-learningtool
Journal of ovarian research 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Perioperative prediction of adnexal malignancy by an interpretable machine learning model for guiding ovarian preservation in premenopausal endometrial cancer.

This study developed an interpretable machine learning model to predict adnexal malignancy in premenopausal endometrial cancer patients, aiding decisions about ovarian preservation during surgery. The model helps identify high-risk patients, potentially allowing more personalized surgical approaches.

machine-learningcancerclinical
Clinical nutrition ESPEN 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Association of serum albumin redox state with dietary protein intake and frailty in older adults: A cross-sectional study.

This study examines whether the fraction of mercaptoalbumin in serum albumin (f(HMA)) can serve as a biomarker linking dietary protein intake to frailty in older adults. The findings suggest that f(HMA) reflects frailty severity and may help explain the relationship between protein intake and physical function. This work highlights a potential new biomarker for assessing protein nutritional status in aging populations.

agingclinicalproteomics
Briefings in bioinformatics 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Bridging local-global transmembrane protein contexts with contrastive pretraining for alignment-free pathogenicity prediction.

Memo-Patho is a deep learning framework that predicts whether mutations in transmembrane proteins are pathogenic, without needing evolutionary sequence alignments. It uses a novel contrastive pretraining approach that learns both local and global protein contexts, outperforming existing methods on this clinically important class of drug targets.

deep-learningmachine-learningprotein-structure
Briefings in bioinformatics 2026-Jul-03 Medium

EssTFNet: integration of adaptive time-frequency and DNA language models for interpretable human essential gene prediction.

EssTFNet is a new deep learning framework that predicts essential genes by combining adaptive time-frequency analysis with DNA language models. It provides interpretable predictions, helping researchers understand which sequence features contribute to gene essentiality, which is important for identifying therapeutic targets and studying evolution.

deep-learninggenomicstool
Briefings in bioinformatics 2026-Jul-03 Medium

DNA-DETR: sequence representation matters in object detection for functional genomic elements.

The authors adapted the DETR object detection architecture for genomic sequence analysis, but found that standard one-hot encoding performed poorly for detecting Non-B DNA structures. They discovered that sequence representation is critical, and that one-hot encoding fails to capture key structural features needed for accurate detection of these genomic elements.

deep-learninggenomicsmethodology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-02 Medium

Automated tracking of the brown algal parasite Eurychasma dicksonii with deep learning.

This study introduces a deep learning-based method to automatically track the brown algal parasite Eurychasma dicksonii in time-lapse microscopy videos, replacing slow and error-prone manual observation. The authors compared five segmentation architectures to identify the best approach for parasite segmentation and multi-object tracking, enabling real-time analysis.

deep-learningmicrobiologytool
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

ProMeta: a meta-learning framework for robust disease diagnosis and prediction from plasma proteomics.

ProMeta is a new meta-learning framework designed to improve disease diagnosis and prediction from plasma proteomics data, even when labeled patient samples are scarce. It addresses the challenge of applying deep learning to rare or novel diseases by enabling models to learn effectively from limited data.

proteomicsmachine-learningmethodology
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

A novel transformer model of protein domains for viral taxonomy classification.

This paper introduces a transformer-based model that uses protein domain information to classify viruses across multiple taxonomic ranks, addressing the challenge that most sequenced viruses lack formal taxonomic assignments. The approach can place novel viruses into higher-level taxa even when they are too divergent for standard sequence-similarity methods, improving classification at ranks above species or genus.

machine-learningdeep-learningmicrobiology
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

USP-ddG: a unified structural paradigm with data efficacy and mixture-of-experts for predicting mutational effects on protein-protein interactions.

This paper introduces USP-ddG, a deep learning method that predicts how mutations affect protein-protein binding strength (ΔΔG) more efficiently by using a unified structural representation and a mixture-of-experts approach, which reduces computational cost while improving accuracy. The model addresses limitations of previous methods that require expensive sampling or generative modeling, and it also optimizes training data organization for better data efficacy.

deep-learningprotein-structuretool
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

NPBIP: predicting binding preferences of uncharacterized nucleic-acid-binding proteins.

NPBIP is a new computational tool that predicts how any nucleic-acid-binding protein (NBP) will bind to RNA or DNA sequences, even if no prior experimental data exists for that protein. It overcomes limitations of existing methods that require pre-existing binding data or can only handle short sequences. This could accelerate the study of uncharacterized NBPs involved in gene regulation.

toolmachine-learninggenomics
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

PU-GRAIL: residue-level graph learning for identifying protective bacterial antigens under positive-unlabeled supervision.

PU-GRAIL is a new computational method that predicts protective bacterial antigens by analyzing protein structures at the residue level, using a graph neural network that learns from positive and unlabeled data. This approach addresses the challenge of sparse annotations in vaccine target discovery by modeling how surface-accessible residues form epitopes, and it handles the inherent uncertainty in negative labeling common in antigen datasets.

machine-learningstructural-biologyimmunology
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

OrgNet+: towards robust protein stability prediction with convolutional neural networks.

This study introduces OrgNet+, a 3D convolutional neural network that improves protein stability prediction by accounting for conformational flexibility, addressing a key limitation of existing structure-based methods that rely on single static structures. The findings show that incorporating ensemble conformations leads to more robust and accurate predictions of mutation effects on protein stability.

machine-learningdeep-learningprotein-structure
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

seq2ribo: structure-aware integration of machine learning and simulation to predict ribosome location profiles from RNA sequences.

seq2ribo is a new method that combines machine learning and simulation to predict ribosome location profiles directly from RNA sequences, without needing experimental data like Ribo-seq or full genomic context. This enables its use in de novo sequence design, such as for mRNA vaccines, overcoming limitations of current methods that require such data or oversimplify translation dynamics.

machine-learningmethodologysynthetic-biology
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 High

Agentomics: an agentic system that autonomously develops novel state-of-the-art solutions for biomedical machine learning tasks.

Agentomics is a new AI agent system that can autonomously design and build machine learning solutions for biomedical problems, outperforming both existing automated ML tools and human-engineered models. It combines large language models with flexible automation to create reproducible, state-of-the-art codebases for tasks like drug discovery and biological data analysis.

machine-learningLLMdrug-discovery
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

MiRformer: a dual-transformer-encoder framework for predicting microRNA-mRNA interactions from paired sequences.

MiRformer is a new deep learning model that uses a dual-transformer-encoder architecture to predict which microRNAs bind to which messenger RNAs directly from their sequences. It overcomes limitations of previous methods by handling long mRNA sequences and providing interpretable predictions of binding sites. This could improve our understanding of gene regulation and aid in developing RNA-based therapies.

deep-learningtranscriptomicstool
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-01 Medium

eGoT: enhanced graph-of-thoughts for multi-hop knowledge retrieval and hypothesis generation in biomedicine.

This paper introduces eGoT, a method that enhances graph-of-thoughts reasoning for multi-hop knowledge retrieval and hypothesis generation in biomedicine. It addresses limitations of large language models by improving retrieval-augmented generation with better provenance tracking and richer responses from fragmented biomedical literature.

machine-learningLLMmethodology
BMC oral health 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Deep learning-based dental age classification from panoramic radiographs: a multi-granularity, multi-model comparative study.

This study evaluates a hybrid deep learning model (MaxViT-T) for estimating dental age and classifying age groups from panoramic dental X-rays, finding it performs well for both fine-grained and coarse-grained tasks. The authors also use explainable AI techniques to interpret the model's decisions, which could aid in clinical applications like forensic age estimation.

deep-learningmachine-learningclinical
PNAS nexus 2026-Jul-01 Medium

A transformer-based language model reveals developmental constraint and network complexity during zebrafish embryogenesis.

This study introduces Zebraformer, a transformer-based language model trained on single-cell transcriptomic data from zebrafish embryos, to test the developmental hourglass hypothesis. The model learns context-sensitive representations that capture temporal progression, anatomical identity, and regulatory relationships, revealing that mid-embryogenesis is a period of heightened conservation and coordinated regulatory organization. This work demonstrates how deep learning can uncover developmental constraints and network complexity from single-cell data.

deep-learningsingle-celldevelopment
mAbs 2026-Dec-31 Medium

Predicting antibody self-association with sequence-structure fusion models: the central role of CSI-BLI in early developability screening.

This study demonstrates that CSI-BLI, a low-material assay measuring weak antibody self-association, serves as an effective early proxy for high-concentration viscosity and in vivo clearance. Using a panel of 246 antibodies, the authors developed sequence-structure fusion models that predict CSI-BLI scores, enabling early developability screening for antibody-based biologics.

machine-learningdrug-discoverytool
PloS one 2026--01 Medium

Discovery of hub genes linking oxidative stress to type 2 diabetic sarcopenia using single-cell sequencing and machine learning.

This study identifies key genes linking oxidative stress to type 2 diabetes-associated sarcopenia by combining single-cell and bulk RNA sequencing with machine learning. The findings highlight potential molecular targets for understanding and treating muscle loss in diabetic patients.

transcriptomicssingle-cellmachine-learning
PloS one 2026--01 Medium

SST subseasonal-to-seasonal forecasting: A heterogeneous three-path fusion model with wavelet decomposition.

This paper presents WDFormer, a deep learning model that improves subseasonal-to-seasonal sea surface temperature (SST) forecasting by decomposing SST data into components using wavelet analysis and fusing them through a three-path architecture. The model achieves accurate daily SST predictions for ENSO monitoring regions using local grid observations, supporting climate and environmental assessment.

deep-learningmethodologyclimate
Neuro-oncology advances 2026--01 Medium

AI-based selection of tumor regions for genomic profiling in neuropathology.

This paper presents an AI method that uses deep learning to automatically identify diagnostically relevant tumor regions from H&E-stained slides of glioma cases, trained on sparse annotations. The model accurately predicts high-cellularity or high-grade areas, reducing the annotation burden and providing reproducible guidance for tissue selection in genomic profiling. Prospective evaluation shows that AI-selected regions are suitable for clinical use.

machine-learningdeep-learningcancer
Frontiers in endocrinology 2026--01 Medium

Development of a risk stratification tool for rapidly progressive diabetic retinopathy in type 2 diabetes.

This study developed a nomogram that combines clinical and biomarker data (diabetes duration, HbA1c, urinary protein, GDF15, DRSS grade, and foveal avascular zone area) to predict the risk of rapidly progressive diabetic retinopathy in type 2 diabetes patients. The tool aims to enable individualized risk stratification, potentially improving early intervention and management.

clinicaltoolmethodology
Frontiers in bioinformatics 2026--01 Medium

CMA-DTI: a cross-modal fusion and attentive interaction network for interpretable drug-target interaction prediction.

This paper presents CMA-DTI, a deep learning model that combines drug molecular graphs and SMILES sequences to predict drug-target interactions more accurately. The model uses cross-modal fusion and attention mechanisms to identify which parts of the drug and target are likely to interact, making the predictions more interpretable for drug discovery.

deep-learningdrug-discoverymachine-learning
Drug design, development and therapy 2026--01 Medium

Swarm Intelligence in Drug Discovery Applications: Unlocking Deeper Insights on the Identification and Optimization of Potential Drug Candidates.

This review explores how swarm intelligence algorithms, inspired by collective behaviors in nature like ant colonies and bee swarms, can be applied to drug discovery tasks such as molecular docking, drug screening, and molecular design. It covers foundational concepts, major algorithms, and hybrid frameworks that combine swarm methods with other computational techniques. The paper serves as a useful overview for researchers interested in nature-inspired optimization for pharmaceutical development.

drug-discoverymachine-learningreview

AI-curated by bioHot. Summaries and significance ratings are AI-generated; verify before citing. · bioHot Home
